edges.description: The BRAF V600E lesion constitutively engages MAPK signaling in HCL cells.
Persistent MAPK signaling helps maintain the survival program of leukemic hairy cells.
edges.target: Apoptosis resistance of leukemic hairy cells

edges.description: Hairy-cell-derived TGF-beta1 drives fibrotic remodeling of the marrow stroma.
Fibrotic and infiltrated marrow supports impaired production of multiple blood lineages.

nodes.description: BRAF-driven signaling promotes an antiapoptotic state that helps malignant hairy cells persist within blood, marrow, and splenic compartments.
nodes.id: Apoptosis resistance of leukemic hairy cells

evidence.snippet: By aberrantly activating the RAF-MEK-ERK signaling pathway, BRAF-V600E shapes key biologic features of HCL, including its specific expression signature, hairy morphology, and antiapoptotic behavior.

nodes.description: Classic HCL is defined by a clonal somatic BRAF V600E mutation that acts as the dominant upstream oncogenic lesion across nearly the entire disease course.

evidence.snippet: The BRAF V600E mutation was present in all patients with HCL who were evaluated.

Hairy cell leukemia (HCL) is a chronic mature B-cell neoplasm with unique clinicopathologic features and an initial exquisite sensitivity to chemotherapy with purine analogs; however, the disease relapses, often repeatedly. The enigmatic pathogenesis of HCL was recently clarified by the discovery of its underlying genetic cause, the BRAF-V600E kinase-activating mutation, which is somatically and clonally present in almost all patients through the entire disease spectrum and clinical course.

nodes.description: Marrow reticulin fibrosis is a characteristic stromal consequence of HCL and contributes to abnormal marrow architecture.

evidence.reference_title: TGF-beta1 induces bone marrow reticulin fibrosis in hairy cell leukemia.
evidence.snippet: Active TGF-beta1 correlated significantly with grades of BM fibrosis, infiltration with HCs, and serum procollagen type III aminoterminal propeptide (PIIINP).

nodes.description: The oncogenic BRAF lesion drives persistent activation of the RAF-MEK-ERK cascade in classic HCL cells.

evidence.snippet: HCL cells expressed phosphorylated MEK and ERK (the downstream targets of the BRAF kinase), indicating a constitutive activation of the RAF-MEK-ERK mitogen-activated protein kinase pathway in HCL.

nodes.description: Classic HCL commonly manifests as multilineage cytopenia, especially pancytopenia, monocytopenia, neutropenia, and thrombocytopenia.

evidence.reference_title: Consensus guidelines for the diagnosis and management of patients with classic hairy cell leukemia.
evidence.snippet: Hairy cell leukemia is an uncommon hematologic malignancy characterized by pancytopenia and marked susceptibility to infection.

evidence.reference_title: Hairy cell leukemia - etiopathogenesis, diagnosis and modern therapeutic approach.
evidence.snippet: In the basic blood count examination, leukopenia with monocytopenia and granulocytopenia, as well as aplastic anemia and/or thrombocytopenia occur.

nodes.description: Hairy cells overproduce TGF-beta1 within marrow and splenic compartments, creating a local profibrotic cytokine environment.

evidence.reference_title: TGF-beta1 induces bone marrow reticulin fibrosis in hairy cell leukemia.
evidence.snippet: RT-PCR and immunofluorescence studies showed that TGF-beta1 is overexpressed at the mRNA and protein levels in peripheral blood, spleen, and BM mononuclear cells and that hairy cells (HCs) are the main source of TGF-beta1.

nodes.description: For relapsed or refractory classic HCL, combined BRAF inhibition and anti-CD20 therapy achieves high complete-response and MRD-negative response rates without myelotoxic chemotherapy.

evidence.reference_title: Vemurafenib plus Rituximab in Refractory or Relapsed Hairy-Cell Leukemia.
evidence.snippet: A complete response was observed in 26 patients (87%) in the intention-to-treat population.
